title: Project Telos Eleventh-Wave Grand-Problem Isolation Claims

claims.text: The eleventh-wave package selects Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness as the primary grand target for the next focused Project Telos proof-packet phase without claiming the grand problem is solved.
claims.falsification: The package is missing, does not select Navier-Stokes as the primary target, or claims the Navier-Stokes Millennium problem is solved.

claims.text: The eleventh-wave source-router demotion gate covers 71 routed rows across 13 lanes and classifies them as 66 domain leads, 3 adjacent leads, 1 query-noise row, and 1 zero-retained-row negative search receipt.
claims.falsification: The demotion gate is missing, row coverage differs from 71, lane count differs from 13, or the class counts differ.

claims.text: The eleventh-wave official-copy draft defines a Navier-Stokes proof-packet program whose first publishable target is a bounded PDE subclaim packet, not a Millennium-solution announcement.
claims.falsification: The official-copy draft is missing, omits the bounded first-packet program, or tells readers a Millennium solution exists.

claims.text: The eleventh-wave content queue includes explicit do-not-post boundaries against claiming a Navier-Stokes solution, a new law of physics, theorem proof from numerical evidence, or accepted publication status.
claims.falsification: The content queue is missing or omits those do-not-post boundaries.

claims.text: The eleventh-wave Forum/Index receipt routes the work to Project Telos and preserves a concrete Index context-envelope MATCH for the local Telos workspace.
claims.falsification: The route receipt is missing, Forum does not route to project-telos, or the Index envelope verdict is not MATCH.

claims.text: The eleventh-wave package treats sidecar hard-problem candidates as backlog and keeps the focused publication path on Navier-Stokes plus bounded proof-carrying PDE subclaims.
claims.falsification: The package promotes sidecar candidates as solved problems or moves the primary publication path away from the selected Navier-Stokes proof-packet program without a replacement decision.
